resources.string:
Query the incoming number online in real time.

Multiple API endpoints will be queried simultaneously, with the first result received being used and all later results ignored.

It has the lowest priority by default, meaning it will be triggered only after the call has passed all local rules.

Default Priority: -1
It will be identified as spam number if this regex pattern is found in the result.

It will be identified as non-spam number if this regex pattern is found in the result, it\'s optional.

Usually this is unnecessary, some API providers will identify unknown numbers as non-spam, only use this when the API is really accurate.

Extract the category and comments from the query result and display them in the notification and history log. (optional)

For example, for this query result:
{
  \"type\": \"MARKETING\",
  \"comments\": \"Better call Saul\",
  …
}

- Extract the category only:
  \"type\": \"(.+?)\"

- Extract both the category and the comments:
  (?:"type":"(.+?)"|"comments":"(.+?)")

Parse the query result, the number will be identified as spam if the result contains a particular regex pattern.

An extra regex can be used to extract the spam category. (optional)

The incoming call number will be parsed into different tags:

%s
These tags can be used in subsequent HTTP requests.
The incoming SMS content will be parsed into tag {sms}, which can be used in subsequent HTTP requests.

Some API services only support international numbers, while some only support domestic numbers.

This regex filters numbers accordingly, incoming numbers that don\'t match this regex will be skipped checking.

- Only check international numbers:
  \\+.*

- Only check domestic numbers:
  0{1,2}.*

- Check any number:
  .*
Android version is lower than 11, skip.
